title: Agent Reflection Loop with Self-Critique
source: CrewAI agent reflection pattern + AutoGen 0.4 nested chat self-verification + LangGraph reflexion pattern (2025-2026)
description: Currently, agents execute once and return output — there is no self-verification step. Modern multi-agent frameworks implement a 'reflection loop' where an agent reviews its own output against acceptance criteria before marking work complete. CrewAI and AutoGen both show 20-40% quality improvement from a single reflection pass. For the CTO Orchestrator, adding an optional reflection step (a cheap Haiku call that checks agent output against the ticket's acceptance_criteria) would catch common issues like missing files, incomplete implementations, or criteria drift before the reviewer-morty stage.
proposed_changes: 1. Add a `reflect_on_output()` function in delegate.py that takes the agent's output and the ticket's acceptance_criteria, calls `claude -p --model haiku` with a prompt like 'Review this agent output against these acceptance criteria. Return JSON {"criteria_met": [list], "criteria_missed": [list], "pass": bool}'. 2. In the main delegation flow (after agent completes), call reflect_on_output(). If pass=false and missed criteria exist, re-invoke the agent with the reflection feedback appended to the prompt (max 1 retry to avoid loops). 3. Add a --no-reflect flag to delegate.py for cases where reflection should be skipped (e.g. dry-run, simple tasks). 4. Log reflection results to the ticket's agent_output for reviewer-morty visibility.
